things.name: Abbey of Saint Gall (UNESCO)
things.description: UNESCO World Heritage Carolingian monastery complex with one of Europe's greatest baroque cathedral churches, founded in the 7th century

things.name: Abbey Library (Stiftsbibliothek)
things.description: UNESCO-listed baroque library of 1767 housing over 170,000 volumes including 2,100 precious medieval manuscripts; one of the world's oldest

things.name: Old Town (Altstadt)
things.description: Charming pedestrianised old town with 111 richly decorated oriel windows projecting from medieval merchant guild houses

things.name: Kunst Halle Sankt Gallen
things.description: Leading contemporary art institution presenting innovative exhibitions in a converted textile hall in the heart of the old town

things.name: Naturmuseum St. Gallen
things.description: Natural history museum with extensive geological, zoological and mineralogical collections representing eastern Switzerland's rich landscapes

things.name: Textile Museum
things.description: Museum celebrating St. Gallen's world-famous embroidery and lace tradition, displayed in period rooms with intricate historic pieces

things.name: Stadtpark
things.description: Elegant Victorian city park with the Historical Museum, greenhouse and bandstand; the green heart of St. Gallen for weekend relaxation

things.name: Peter and Paul Wildlife Park
things.description: Free hilltop wildlife park above the city where Alpine animals including ibex, red deer and chamois roam in large natural enclosures

things.name: Drei Weieren
things.description: Three scenic ponds on the hillside above St. Gallen offering swimming, walking trails and panoramic views to Lake Constance and the Alps

things.name: Kybunpark Stadium
things.description: Home of FC St. Gallen, Switzerland's oldest football club founded in 1879, with a 20,000-capacity modern arena
